description: Tagging system guidelines — domain-specific patterns, prohibited generics, aliases policy, cross-reference enforcement.

policy.rule: All pages MUST have 3-7 tags. No generic/broad terms alone.

aliases_system.description: Discoverability synonyms — what users actually type in queries
include: Common synonyms ('LLM Wiki Pattern' for Loomana)
Bilingual variants if used by user (EN/RU)
exclude: Architecture terms — these are tags, not aliases

cross_reference_enforcement.rule: If page A links to page B → both must share at least one common tag
cross_reference_enforcement.enforcement: process-ingest.json#post_check: validate shared tags before writing crosslinks

policy.rule: Use EITHER English OR Russian within one document — never mix languages in tags
policy.recommendation: English preferred for consistency with AGENTS.md conventions
exceptions: Russian-only content pages may use ru-tags exclusively

language_consistency.section_headers: All section titles in templates are English — ## Definition, ## Key Characteristics, etc. Provides consistent structural anchors regardless of content language.
language_consistency.page_content_language: Content follows source language. Bilingual sources -> bilingual sections (allowed and normal). No forced translation required at ingest time.
language_consistency.agent_response_translation: When synthesizing answer: translate section headers to match user's question language. Content paraphrasing is agent's discretion — can quote directly, summarize, or translate.
language_consistency.mixed_language_pages: Allowed and encouraged when reflecting bilingual sources. Agent treats each section independently for translation at response time.

actions.action: error: inline comments break YAML parsing

primary_name_prefix_mandatory.rule: When creating documents (docs/, concepts/) belonging to an entity, ALWAYS use the entity's PRIMARY NAME as file prefix. NEVER use aliases.
primary_name_prefix_mandatory.rationale: Aliases are discoverability synonyms in frontmatter `aliases: []`. They may not match user search patterns and can cause cross-link failures.

example.wrong_prefix: wiki/docs/loom-architecture.md (alias used instead of primary name)

primary_name_prefix_mandatory.agent_instruction: Before creating any related doc: check entity page's tags — first tag = PRIMARY NAME. Use that as prefix, not aliases.

parent_entity_identification.rule: Page's parent entity = what the page is PRIMARILY ABOUT, not the technology it uses or depends on.

heuristics.condition: source is entity's own official docs/site
source discusses using technology X within project Y
heuristics.parent: project Y (the user of the technology)
heuristics.example: "Using Symfony Messenger in MyApp" → parent = MyApp, Symfony = related tag[1]
heuristics.rationale: The page documents the project's configuration, not the technology itself

heuristics.condition: source is a general guide/tutorial about technology X
page title contains both project and technology names
heuristics.parent: the entity named first in the title

parent_entity_identification.fallback: If heuristics are ambiguous: ask user which entity should be tag[0] before creating the page.
parent_entity_identification.effect_on_tag_ordering: parent entity's primary name → tag[0]. All related entities/technologies → tags[1+].
parent_entity_identification.effect_on_naming: parent entity's primary name → file prefix (wiki/docs/<parent>-<topic>.md).

effect_on_frontmatter.related: parent entity page goes into `related: []`. Related technologies also go into `related: []`.
effect_on_frontmatter.aliases: aliases of the parent entity (not related technologies).

parent_entity_identification.agent_instruction: Before creating any doc page: first identify the parent entity. Ask: 'What is this page ABOUT?' not 'What does this reference?' Documentation about USING a tool within a project → project is parent. Documentation OF a tool itself → tool is parent.

tag_ordering_for_entity_docs.rule: Every document belonging to an entity MUST have the entity's primary name as its FIRST tag and alias (if used) as SECOND tag.
tag_ordering_for_entity_docs.rationale: Tag order determines search priority. First tag = highest weight in index lookup. Entity name first ensures the page is found when searching for that entity, regardless of file naming conventions.

fallback_on_violation.scenario: If a doc file was created using entity's alias as prefix (e.g., `loom-*.md` instead of `Loomana-*.md`) — this violates the primary_name_prefix_mandatory rule. The entity docs MUST compensate by ensuring: tag[0] = entity primary name, tag[1] = alias used in prefix.
fallback_on_violation.rationale: This ensures cross-searchability even when naming convention was violated. Users searching for 'Loomana' will find the page via first tag, even if file is named with alias prefix.

example.violation: uses 'loom' (alias) as prefix instead of 'Loomana' (primary name)
example.compensation_tags: [Loomana, loom, architecture] — tag 0 = primary name, tag 1 = alias used in filename

tag_ordering_for_entity_docs.agent_instruction: When creating entity docs: first tag = entity's PRIMARY NAME (from entity page tags). Second tag = any alias relevant to this doc. Never put aliases before the primary name.
